name: Idaho SNAP Food Assistance
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to eligible low-income Idaho households through the Department of Health and Welfare.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Idaho Medicaid
description: State Medicaid program providing health coverage for low-income Idahoans including children, families, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled residents through managed care.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, adults up to 138% FPL, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 190% FPL; pregnant women up to 185% FPL

name: Idaho Department of Labor - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial benefits to Idaho workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they actively seek new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Idaho Housing and Finance Association (IHFA)
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable mortgage programs, rental assistance, Section 8 vouchers, homelessness prevention, and down payment assistance statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: Idaho Legal Aid Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Idahoans statewide in matters including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer protection, and elder law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Idaho residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Idaho Department of Labor - Workforce Development
description: State workforce development offices providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, training referrals, and employer connections across Idaho.
eligibilitySummary: Any Idaho resident seeking employment or career development services

name: Idaho LIHEAP - Home Energy Assistance
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helping eligible Idaho households pay heating and cooling bills through the Department of Health and Welfare.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: Idaho 211 - Idaho CareLine
description: Free statewide information and referral service connecting Idahoans with health and human services resources including food, housing, healthcare, and utility assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Any Idaho resident seeking help or information about available services

name: The Idaho Foodbank
description: Feeding America affiliate and the state's largest hunger-relief organization distributing food through hundreds of partner agencies including pantries, shelters, and schools statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance across Idaho

name: Idaho Division of Veterans Services
description: Assists Idaho veterans and their families with benefits claims, healthcare referrals, education, employment, and access to the Idaho State Veterans Home.
eligibilitySummary: Idaho veterans and their families

name: Idaho Suicide Prevention Hotline
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support, crisis intervention, and referrals for individuals experiencing suicidal thoughts or emotional distress.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Idaho experiencing a mental health crisis, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress

name: Idaho Child Care Program (ICCP)
description: Subsidized childcare program helping low-income working Idaho families afford quality childcare while they work or attend job training.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under 13 needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 130% of federal poverty level at initial eligibility

name: Idaho WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and health referrals.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Idaho TAFI Cash Assistance
description: Temporary Assistance for Families in Idaho providing cash benefits and support services to very low-income families with dependent children.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below TAFI payment standard for family size

name: Valley Regional Transit - Boise Area
description: Public transit system serving the Boise metropolitan area including Boise, Nampa, Meridian, and Caldwell with fixed routes and paratransit.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Boise metro area; reduced fare for seniors, disabled, and youth

name: Idaho Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income Idaho households including insulation, air sealing, and heating system repairs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Idaho GED / Adult Education
description: Free adult basic education services including GED preparation, English language learning, and basic literacy classes offered through community programs statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school

name: Idaho Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and shelter referrals for survivors of domestic violence throughout Idaho.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence or sexual assault in Idaho

name: Boise City/Ada County Housing Authorities
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in the Boise and Ada County area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Boise and Ada County
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income for public housing; 50% for vouchers

name: Idaho Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services for Idahoans with disabilities including vocational counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology to prepare for work.
eligibilitySummary: Idaho residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact their ability to work

name: Idaho CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Provides health coverage for uninsured children in Idaho families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private health insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income too high for Medicaid
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 190% of federal poverty level

name: Your Health Idaho - State Insurance Exchange
description: Idaho's state-based health insurance marketplace where residents can compare and enroll in qualified health plans with potential premium subsidies.
eligibilitySummary: Idaho residents without employer coverage or other qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL
hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM MT; extended during open enrollment

name: Idaho Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income Idahoans to ensure access to essential telecommunications services.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: Terry Reilly Health Services - Treasure Valley
description: Federally qualified health center serving the Boise Treasure Valley with affordable primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Treasure Valley area; sliding fee scale for uninsured patients
incomeLimitNotes: Sliding fee scale based on income and family size

name: Idaho Opportunity Scholarship
description: State-funded scholarship for Idaho residents attending eligible Idaho colleges and universities to help make higher education more affordable.
eligibilitySummary: Idaho residents enrolled at eligible in-state institutions with financial need
incomeLimitNotes: Must demonstrate financial need through FAFSA

name: Idaho Community Action Network
description: Network of community action agencies providing emergency assistance, case management, and self-sufficiency programs for low-income Idaho residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Idaho residents seeking emergency assistance or self-sufficiency programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Idaho Commission on Aging
description: Provides services for seniors including meal programs, caregiver support, transportation assistance, and benefits counseling for elderly Idahoans.
eligibilitySummary: Idaho residents age 60 and older and their caregivers
incomeLimitNotes: Some services have income requirements; others available to all seniors

name: Idaho Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers
description: Federally funded rental assistance vouchers administered through IHFA and local housing authorities to help very low-income Idaho families afford housing.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Generally household income at or below 50% of area median income

name: Idaho Legal Aid - Volunteer Lawyers Program
description: Pro bono program matching low-income Idahoans with volunteer attorneys for civil legal matters at no cost through clinics and direct representation.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Idaho residents with civil legal needs who cannot afford an attorney
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Jesse Tree of Idaho - Boise Homeless Prevention
description: Nonprofit providing emergency rent and utility assistance to prevent homelessness for at-risk families and individuals in the Boise area.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families at risk of homelessness in the Boise/Ada County area
incomeLimitNotes: Must be at or below 30% area median income
